user_prompt: for anything that is a directly registered callback function from `main.cpp`, move them there
agent_reply: Done. I moved the directly registered callback functions out of `main.cpp` into [src/main_callbacks.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main_callbacks.cpp), with declarations in [inc/main_callbacks.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/main_callbacks.h).

I also updated [src/all_init.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp) and [lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp) to include the new callback header instead of carrying local `extern` declarations. `main.cpp` now keeps the shared flow/helper functions and state that the callbacks use.
Verification: `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passed. It only emitted the existing third-party `NetworkServer::available()` deprecation warning.

commit_message: code neatened, remove cloud features with a predefine

user_prompt: clean up bugs I've introduced when I wrote in `BUILD_CLOUD_FEATURES`
agent_reply: - Fixed a real overflow in `WifiManager::sanitize_network_config()` when cloud is disabled: it was iterating cloud entries using the station count.
- Made cloud-disabled builds use `CLOUD_SERVER_CNT_ALLOWED = 0` while keeping a legal one-slot storage array.
- Kept `DiskStats::refreshRecordingUploadStats()` scanning local recordings even when cloud upload is disabled.
- Made cloud-history pruning a clean no-op `Done` result when cloud is disabled.
- Cleaned `WebCfgHandlers` so disabled cloud config returns/parses as `cloud_uploads:false` without awkward `#ifdef` expression bugs.
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passed with cloud disabled.
- Temporarily enabled `BUILD_CLOUD_FEATURES`, rebuilt `pio run -e thefly_sec0`, and it passed too.
- Restored the switch to commented-out afterward, and restored generated `src/version.c`.
- Fixed a real overflow in `WifiManager::sanitize_network_config()` when cloud is disabled: it was iterating cloud entries using the station count.
- Made cloud-disabled builds use `CLOUD_SERVER_CNT_ALLOWED = 0` while keeping a legal one-slot storage array.
- Kept `DiskStats::refreshRecordingUploadStats()` scanning local recordings even when cloud upload is disabled.
- Made cloud-history pruning a clean no-op `Done` result when cloud is disabled.
- Cleaned `WebCfgHandlers` so disabled cloud config returns/parses as `cloud_uploads:false` without awkward `#ifdef` expression bugs.
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passed with cloud disabled.
- Temporarily enabled `BUILD_CLOUD_FEATURES`, rebuilt `pio run -e thefly_sec0`, and it passed too.
- Restored the switch to commented-out afterward, and restored generated `src/version.c`.

user_prompt: Wire it up, with a ConnWaitingView with the same NTP graphic, with a confirmation or error dialog at the end, and allow for it to be cancelled
agent_reply: - Displays `Syncing time:` with the primary configured NTP server.
- Can be cancelled from the waiting view, returning to the previous scroll view without later popping a result dialog.
- Shows a success dialog with RTC update status and offset, or an error dialog with the failure reason.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`. Build passes.

commit_message: major progress on usability, added ntp sync

user_prompt: add a `ScrollView::populateFiles` function, along with enums to give it a `File List` context
it uses `lib\AudioFileRecorder\MostRecentFiles.cpp`, to populate up to 16 most recent files
it uses `sprite_record_100` for `.wav` files, these are the only type that needs a callback, make the callback a placeholder for now, but later, we will implement a playback function with a view
it uses `sprite_recordenc_100` for `.rec` files, clicking causes no reaction

it uses `sprite_fwupdate` for files matching `firmware.bin`, clicking on it causes the screen to immediately fade (the horizontal stripe routine) and then reboot (which will trigger a firmware update)
it uses `sprite_fileunknown_100` for all other file types, clicking causes no reaction
the file names are displayed as the text associated with the item
also insert a button with `sprite_info_100` for getting a summary of information. This dialog is already implemented. Reuse it
right now, on the `MainScreenView`, the top center item is the information item, switch it out for showing this new `ScrollView` with the file list context, use the icon `sprite_floppy_100`

commit_message: hotel can shutdown if playback is paused

user_prompt: When Wi-Fi either establishes a connection or starts a soft AP, lower the GUI's polling rate to the lowest setting
agent_reply: Wi-Fi connection state now drives GUI polling through callbacks:

I also updated `FlyGui` so Wi-Fi activity forces `FLYGUI_POLL_SLOW`, and audio can no longer accidentally restore `FAST` while Wi-Fi is active. When Wi-Fi is inactive, audio still maps to `MEDIUM`, otherwise it returns to `FAST`.
Note: `inc/web_assets.h` is still modified from PlatformIO’s asset packaging step, and `src/version.c` plus the untracked files were already in the worktree.

user_prompt: that's weird, we have existing event handling for these, I need this change redone, I don't want too many event handlers for the same event
agent_reply: Redone. I removed the extra Wi-Fi connect/disconnect callbacks and registrations.
Now the slow GUI polling is triggered from the existing flow points:
I kept the `FlyGui::setWifiActive()` state so Wi-Fi still wins over audio polling changes and forces `FLYGUI_POLL_SLOW`.
